description_long: This is a two-story brick factory building with a rectangular footprint and a gabled, shingle-textured roof. The facade features multiple large, industrial-style windows arranged in rows, providing ample natural light to the interior. A prominent covered loading dock extends from the front, supported by two posts, and a secondary, open-sided canopy is attached to one side, likely for vehicle or freight access. The exterior walls are primarily composed of red-brown brick, with dark trim around the windows and roof edges. There are no visible smokestacks or rooftop machinery, and the building's design is utilitarian, emphasizing function over ornamentation. The overall appearance suggests early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture, suitable for manufacturing or warehousing.
description: A two-story brick factory building with a gabled roof, large industrial windows, and a covered loading dock. The structure is utilitarian and functional, with red-brown brick walls and dark trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a covered loading dock and two supporting posts, as well as upper and lower rows of industrial windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the building, the main loading dock, and a secondary open-sided canopy on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a solid brick wall with a single door and a row of upper windows, with the side canopy visible to the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing two large doors, balcony-like ledges, and the open-sided canopy on the right.
